sourceMovie.title: Flubber
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Flubber works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Stitch! The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flubber clicked for you, Stitch! The Movie (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Flubber?
faq.answer: Try Stitch! The Movie, Henry Danger: The Movie, The Croods: A New Age—each shares DNA with Flubber while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Flubber?
faq.answer: Flubber (1997) is best described as Comedy, Family, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Flubber worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
